task_completion.description: Verify the agent visited check.torproject.org and searched the DuckDuckGo onion site for the ransomware topic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser window showing either the 'check.torproject.org' success page or the DuckDuckGo onion site (URL ending in .onion) with the search query 'ransomware groups 2024 threat landscape'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Threat Intel - Ransomware' bookmark folder containing the search results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser's bookmark manager, bookmarks sidebar, or bookmarks toolbar displaying a folder exactly named 'Threat Intel - Ransomware' with at least one saved page inside.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved the top result title to the ransomware research notes file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/ransomware_research_notes.txt' being edited or viewed, containing text related to the ransomware search results.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent used the 'New Identity' feature to ensure a fresh session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser 'New Identity' confirmation dialog box, the broom icon being clicked, or evidence of the browser restarting/clearing its state before the second investigation.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent visited check.torproject.org again on the new circuit and searched the DuckDuckGo onion site for the phishing topic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser window showing 'check.torproject.org' (verifying the new IP) or the DuckDuckGo onion site with the search query 'phishing infrastructure takedown reports 2024'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Threat Intel - Phishing' bookmark folder containing the search results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser's bookmark manager, bookmarks sidebar, or bookmarks toolbar displaying a folder exactly named 'Threat Intel - Phishing' with at least one saved page inside.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved findings to the phishing research notes file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/phishing_research_notes.txt' being edited or viewed, containing text related to the phishing search results.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the Tor Browser GUI to navigate the dark web.
integrity.visual_evidence: The official Tor Browser window (with the onion logo and specific UI elements) is visible, rather than the agent attempting to use terminal commands like curl or standard browsers like Firefox/Chrome which cannot natively resolve .onion addresses.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the browser's UI to create bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bookmark star icon is clicked, or the bookmark library/manager window is open, rather than the agent using terminal commands to directly manipulate the browser's SQLite database.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent extracted real search results into the notes files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the notes files matches the actual search results visible in the browser screenshots, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate or copy-paste generic text from the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create two files: '/home/ga/Documents/ransomware_research_notes.txt' and '/home/ga/Documents/phishing_research_notes.txt', both containing text. The agent must create two bookmark folders exactly named 'Threat Intel - Ransomware' and 'Threat Intel - Phishing'. The browser history must show visits to 'check.torproject.org' and 'duckduckgogg42xjoc72x3sjasowoarfbgcmvfimaftt6twagswzczad.onion'.
